question: Are there countries where methane emissions from the Energy sector show clear decreasing trends between 1990 and 2018?

analysis.design_rationale: A conventional line chart is chosen because it excels at displaying time-series trends for each country. By plotting emission trajectories for top countries in the Energy sector, we allow visual detection of clear decreasing patterns. Highlighting countries with strong negative slopes (based on linear regression from 1990 to 2018) provides actionable insight and guides the eye. Multiple lines facilitate comparison while annotations help point out sharply decreasing cases.
analysis.target_insight: Visual identification of countries in the Energy sector with clear decreasing methane emissions over time and comparison with peers.

chart_encodings.color: Country (highlight those with decreasing trend in bold color, others in pale gray)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: alpha for less decreasing countries, bold line for decreasing trend, title with escape sequence '\n'

transformations: For each Country, calculate linear regression slope for emissions from 1990-2018
Identify countries with negative slope below a threshold (e.g., slope < -0.5)
Select top 6 decreasing countries, also include major emitting countries for contrast
data_preparation_steps: Extract emissions by country and years
For each country: fit np.polyfit(range(1990,2019), emissions, 1) and store slope
Select countries with slope < -0.5, get top 6 by magnitude of decrease
Optionally pick top 2 highest overall emitters for context
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\2.csv")
df_energy = df[df['Sector'] == 'Energy']
year_cols = ['1990','1991','1992','1993','1994','1995','1996','1997','1998','1999','2000','2001','2002','2003','2004','2005','2006','2007','2008','2009','2010','2011','2012','2013','2014','2015','2016','2017','2018']
years = np.array([int(y) for y in year_cols])
slope_dict = {}
for country, group in df_energy.groupby('Country'):
    vals = group[year_cols].values.flatten()
    if np.isnan(vals).any() or np.count_nonzero(vals) < 10:
        continue
    slope, intercept = np.polyfit(years, vals, 1)
    slope_dict[country] = slope
slopes = pd.Series(slope_dict)
decreasing_countries = slopes[slopes < -0.5].sort_values().index[:6]
top_emitters = df_energy.groupby('Country')['2018'].mean().sort_values(ascending=False).index[:2]
selected_countries = list(decreasing_countries) + list(top_emitters)
plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
for country in selected_countries:
    group = df_energy[df_energy['Country'] == country]
    vals = group[year_cols].values.flatten()
    if country in decreasing_countries:
        plt.plot(years, vals, linewidth=2.5, color='crimson', label=country)
    else:
        plt.plot(years, vals, linewidth=1.5, color='gray', alpha=0.5, label=country)
plt.title('Methane Emissions from Energy Sector (1990-2018)\nHighlighted countries with clear decreasing trends', fontsize=15)
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Methane Emissions (MTCO2e)')
plt.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
